title: Remove external redirects and implement in-website authentication

requirements.summary: Audit and remove all external navigation links, ensuring all navigation stays within the single-page application using TanStack Router
acceptanceCriteria: No link or button in the app causes the browser to navigate to an external domain
The SiteFooter 'Built with caffeine.ai' attribution link (if present) either opens in a new tab without leaving the app or is removed
All internal navigation uses TanStack Router <Link> or navigate() rather than raw <a href='https://...'> tags that replace the current page

file_operations.description: Update the 'Built with caffeine.ai' attribution link to open in a new tab (target='_blank' rel='noopener noreferrer') rather than replacing the current page, or remove the link entirely if it redirects users away from the app
Audit all navigation links and buttons in the site header to ensure they use TanStack Router <Link> components or navigate() calls instead of raw anchor tags with external hrefs
Review the Discover and Publish Experience buttons to ensure they navigate using TanStack Router rather than external redirects
Audit all navigation elements, including the recommended experiences grid and friend activity sidebar, to ensure they use internal TanStack Router navigation
Review all category tabs, search results, and publish buttons to ensure they navigate internally via TanStack Router
Ensure the play button and any creator profile links navigate using TanStack Router rather than window.location or external hrefs
Verify that clicking an experience card navigates to the detail page using TanStack Router <Link> or navigate() instead of raw anchor tags

requirements.summary: Replace Internet Identity authentication with in-website modal-based login/signup using the existing session-auth system
acceptanceCriteria: Clicking 'Login' or 'Sign Up' in the SiteHeader or any AuthButtons component opens an in-app login dialog/modal or navigates to an internal route — never redirecting to an external identity provider
The useSessionAuth hook's login and signup methods are wired up as the primary auth mechanism
Internet Identity (AuthClient) is not invoked in a way that redirects the user away from the app
After successful login or signup, the user stays on the same domain and is shown their authenticated state without a full page reload to an external URL
Logout clears the session and returns the user to the home page within the app

file_operations.description: Update Login and Sign Up buttons to navigate to internal /login and /signup routes (or open modal dialogs) instead of triggering Internet Identity authentication that redirects users to identity.ic0.app. Wire these buttons to use TanStack Router navigation or open inline modal components using the session auth system from useSessionAuth

Ensure the Login page uses the session auth hook (useSessionAuth) for authentication and does not invoke Internet Identity or cause any external redirects. After successful login, redirect the user to the home page using TanStack Router navigate()

Ensure the SignUp page uses the session auth hook (useSessionAuth) for registration and does not invoke Internet Identity or cause any external redirects. After successful signup, redirect the user to the home page using TanStack Router navigate()
Update the login/sign-up prompt to navigate to internal /login and /signup routes using TanStack Router <Link> components instead of any external authentication flow
Update the Log In and Sign Up navigation buttons to use TanStack Router <Link> or navigate() to internal routes (/login, /signup) instead of triggering any external authentication redirects

Review and update the session auth hook to ensure login, signup, and logout methods are fully implemented without any Internet Identity or external redirect dependencies. Logout should clear the session token and navigate the user to the home page using TanStack Router
Ensure the useCurrentUser hook relies solely on useSessionAuth for authentication state and does not depend on Internet Identity or any external auth providers
Remove or comment out the InternetIdentityProvider wrapper if it triggers external redirects, ensuring the app bootstraps with only the session-based authentication system

requirements.summary: Update the AuthChangeBanner component to reflect that in-website authentication is now active and remove references to external login redirects being disabled
acceptanceCriteria: AuthChangeBanner no longer references external login redirects as a known limitation
If the banner is no longer needed, it is removed from AppLayout

file_operations.description: Update the banner message to reflect that in-website authentication is now the default behavior and remove any messaging about external login redirects being disabled. If the banner is no longer relevant, replace its content with a simple informational message or remove the component entirely
Review the inclusion of AuthChangeBanner and consider removing it from the layout if it is no longer needed after in-website authentication is implemented
